div.h2: Technical Analysis of zero_grad() side-effects on Memory Buffers

div.p: Gradients represent the local curvature of the surface for the current batch. Independent batch updates allow the optimizer to follow the true stochastic manifold.
The 0.30 from the previous step is physically still in the buffer. backward() performs a += operation, treating Iteration N+1 as having a massive gradient of 0.50. This leads to Gradient Explosion.
